reviews.title: ⚠️ 🔒 🚫 Account Suspended Without Clear Explanation
reviews.content:
I’m disappointed with my experience with Claude.

My account was placed on hold for “suspicious patterns,” and after submitting an appeal, I received a generic response stating that I violated the Usage Policy. However, no specific explanation or policy section was provided.

I used Claude primarily for software development, research, and personal projects. I had important ongoing work and project materials stored in my account, so losing access has had a significant impact.

I understand the need for safety systems, but I believe users deserve more transparency when serious actions such as account suspensions occur. Without knowing what specific activity triggered the decision, it is difficult to understand what went wrong or how to avoid similar issues in the future.

I hope Anthropic improves communication and provides users with more clarity regarding account reviews and enforcement decisions.

——— • ———

Мой аккаунт был переведён в

That is, in fact, I go into the application to help with any question or task, to which the bot replies to me "send me a solution to this problem and I'll figure out how to solve it" damn brilliant 👏👏👏 I tried to change the model, chose maximum efficiency, tried to chat with him in 10 chats during all this time, and each time I had to spend hours explaining what I needed and what his mistake was, but he keeps doing the same thing. “I’ve had a very disappointing experience with Gemini, especially considering that I’m using the paid version of the service and expected a much more stable and consistent experience.

On several occasions while generating images, I noticed results with strong biases that can feel discriminatory or even racist depending on the prompt and context. It’s concerning to see an AI tool behave this inconsistently when interpreting visual requests.

I’m also very frustrated with the video generation feature. I had already used it before with this same account and it worked correctly, but now I keep getting the message saying that it’s ‘not available in your region.’ I’m still in the same region where it previously worked, so it’s unclear whether this is a technical issue, a new restriction, or a change that was never properly communicated.

As a paid user, I expect more transparency about feature availability, greater reliability, and more careful review of how the AI handles image and video g

reviews.title: Money to buy frustrations
reviews.content: I would give it less than 1 if i have the option. I downloaded this ai app and paid ai plus subscription to help develop plots for a novel writing project. It cannot retain or apply information with any complexity. It will answer each request as if the background setting, character design, term definitions shared in previous chats solemnly existed unless you explicitly retype those sections again, which is pure waste of token and user’s time. Imagine, for every prompt, you have to retype information that you already typed 10 times in previous prompt in the same chat thread. It cannot drive any logic above 1st degree from raw information. Most chat replies are composed of 30% seemingly randomly chosen facts from existing user inputs, 40% web search inputs relevant to conversation, 20% made up stuff written as if it was user input(aka putting illogical words in the users mouth), and 10% ridiculous logic that stitches all these components together. The result is a well worded confident re

reviews.title: A useless ia for real emergency , prioritize ideological responses over my dog welfare
reviews.content: The level of disconnection in this application is outrageous. I sought urgent help for my elderly dog, who needed to learn how to use an attractant spray to urinate inside the house due to my work schedule. Instead of analyzing the real problem seriously and providing practical solutions regarding the product, the system got tangled up in robotic responses, assuming false things and forcing a tone that felt more like a textbook political/progressive agenda than actual help for a suffering animal. When it comes to ideological or trendy topics, these systems respond to the millimeter, but when an owner seeks practical assistance for their pet, they throw it to the garbage with cold responses and useless interpretations. A complete piece of garbage programming that is useless for real life.

reviews.title: Zero stars if I could. Google completely ruined it!!!!
reviews.content:
Fitbit used to be one of the best fitness apps available: fast, accurate, simple, and actually useful. Then Google got involved and turned it into bloated, inaccurate garbage.

Google Health’s nutrition database is wildly unreliable, food tracking is clunky, and basic features Fitbit mastered years ago are now buried under layers of bad design. Fitbit let users import custom foods properly, enter exact gram measurements easily, and quickly view heart rate zones and activity minutes without digging through a maze of nonsense. Google somehow managed to make every feature slower, harder, and less intuitive.

This is what happens when a giant company buys a great product and “improves” it until it’s unrecognizable. What used to be a clean, efficient fitness platform is now an overcomplicated mess.

Don’t waste your money on what used to be Fitbit watches. Google turned a great product into complete trash.

reviews.title: Take it easy with AI
